description: Present multi-item proof, resources, or stories in a gallery or carousel that includes explicit buttons or pagination controls instead of relying on hidden gestures alone.
evidence_notes: VoidZero uses a gallery region with numbered slide buttons.
Notion exposes next controls and pause behavior in carousel-like sections.
rules: Always expose visible controls for moving between items.
Use the gallery for proof or resources that benefit from browsing, not for mandatory comprehension.
Keep each card useful as a standalone destination.
implementation_notes: Prefer button-based pagination and pause controls over gesture-only interaction.
Ensure focus and announcements are predictable when slides change.
Allow the content to degrade into a simple list when JavaScript fails.
avoid_when: Critical product information is buried inside slides.
Auto-advance makes the content harder to read.
There are too few items to justify a carousel shell.
